messages.content: True or False: Grant Still's Afro-American Symphony was the most performed work by an American composer for a long time.
False. "Rhapsody in Blue" by George Gershwin was the most performed work by an American composer for a long time.
True or False: Troubled Island is about the Haitian Revolution at the end of the 18th century/beginning of the 19th century.
True. "Troubled Island" is an opera by William Grant Still and Langston Hughes that is indeed about the Haitian Revolution.
true or false: Troubled Island was premiered by the New York City Opera.
False. "Troubled Island" was premiered by the New York City Opera, with a libretto by Langston Hughes and music by William Grant Still.
true or false: William Grant Still attended Oberlin College.
True. William Grant Still did attend Oberlin College. He studied composition and double bass there.
true or false: William Grant Still served in the United States Navy during WWII.
False. William Grant Still did not serve in the United States Navy during WWII. He was exempt from military service due to age and health reasons, as he was in his late 50s at the time.
True or false: Debussy heavily influenced William Grant Still.

True. Debussy heavily influenced William Grant Still's compositional style. Still was particularly inspired by Debussy's use of color, impressionistic harmonies, and incorporation of African-American folk melodies into his works.
True or false: William Grant Still was not widely accepted by his circle of peers in New York City, which included Bernstein and Copland.

False. William Grant Still was widely accepted and respected by his circle of peers in New York City, which included prominent composers such as Leonard Bernstein and Aaron Copland. He was an influential figure in the African-American classical music community and received recognition for his compositions throughout his career.
True or False: William Grant Still was born on May 11, 1895 in Little Rock, AR.
True. William Grant Still was indeed born on May 11, 1895 in Little Rock, Arkansas.
True or False: William Grant Still moved to LA during the 1940's.
True. William Grant Still did move to Los Angeles during the 1940s. He relocated there to pursue various musical opportunities, including composing for film and television.
True or False: Langston Hughes was the sole librettist for Troubled Island.

False. While Langston Hughes was involved in the creation of the libretto for "Troubled Island," he co-wrote it with Verna Arvey. Verna Arvey was an opera singer and composer who collaborated with Hughes on several projects, including this opera.
